sourceMovie.title: Nouvelle Vague
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Nouvelle Vague works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: October Sky
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Nouvelle Vague clicked for you, October Sky (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Moxie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Nouvelle Vague clicked for you, Moxie (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Nouvelle Vague?
faq.answer: Try October Sky, Moxie, Antwone Fisher—each shares DNA with Nouvelle Vague while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Nouvelle Vague?
faq.answer: Nouvelle Vague (2025) is best described as Comedy, Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Nouvelle Vague worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
